text:
End Game (！, Saishū Shiken Kaishi!—lit. "The Final Test Begins!") is the 4 th volume of the Hunter × Hunter manga series. It was released by Shueisha on February 4 th , 1999 in Japan and by Viz Media on September 6 th , 2005 in English.

Chapters

27. A Volatile Situation (, Isshoku-sokuhatsu)
28. A Huge Favor (きなり, Ōkina Kari)
29. Killua's Case (キルアの, Kirua no Baai)
30. The Slithering Trap (く, Ugomeku Wana)
31. By the Skin of Their Teeth... (に..., Kyūshi ni...)
32. And the Final Test...? (は...？, Saishū Shiken wa...?)
33. The Final Test Begins! (！, Saishū Shiken Kaishi!)
34. The First Candidate Accepted?! (1！？, Gōkaku Dai Ichi-gō!?)
35. Light and Darkness: Part 1 (と①, Hikari to Yami (Ichi))

Summary

Gon is both terrified and exhilarated by the challenge of stealing a tag from Hisoka, undoubtedly the most dangerous of the other examinees. He trains for days, perfecting a technique to snatch a tag with his fishing pole's hook. Gon then finds and stalks Hisoka, waiting for him to attack someone so his movements become predictable and his defenses are lowered. Gon carries out his plan flawlessly, but as he escapes from the scene, he is felled by a tranquilizer dart from Geretta, who has been stalking Gon in the same manner and has his tags stolen. Hisoka soon appears before Gon, holding Geretta's head, and gives Gon his tags back, complimenting him on the superb fishing pole technique. Humiliated at having to accept charity, Gon seeks out Kurapika and Leorio after he recovers from the tranquilizer, helping them to obtain their targets' tags. At the end of the week allotted for the Fourth Phase, nine examinees remain.

The final phase is an elimination tournament, with a twist. The winners are removed from the tournament and the losers are given more chances. The only person without a win during the tournament fails the exam, and all other examinees are awarded licenses. The matches themselves are also unique. They can only be won by forcing the opponent to admit defeat. Beating the opponent unconscious will not win a match, and any examinee that kills their opponent will be automatically disqualified and the only one not to pass.

The first round is between Gon and Hanzo. Although Gon is initially optimistic, he quickly finds out that Hanzo's skills dwarf his own. Through merciless beatings and broken bones, Gon refuses to admit defeat. Even threats of death do not faze him. In the end, Hanzo realizes that he cannot win. He surrenders and then knocks Gon unconscious. Gon wakes up after the tournament is over and finds that he is officially a Hunter, but learns that Killua failed after killing someone.
